d2\08-Born in Time.flac:f56a7d1d9acbea76bfb8fb9bcd3c0a14
d2\09-To Ramona.flac:22678cb48f5921c5596f2e03a4f03103
d2\10-God Knows.flac:22ba808a69f6f742f05ae7c569a43c0f
d2\11-Blowin' in the wind.flac:42c803b9a3335d134055a229d36861e9
===================
===================
===================
=== info.txt
===================
===================
===================
Bob Dylan - 1994-02-16 Hiroshima,Japan (RAZ 005/6)

Through A Glass Darkly 

Lineage: received as audio cd-r's > EAC v. 0.99 Prebeta 4 (Secure, Offset Correct) > TLH-FLAC (8) > HC

Disc one
Jokerman
Shelter From The Storm
All Along The Watchtower
She Belongs To Me
Tangled Up In Blue
Watching The River Flow
Tomorrow Night (arr. by Bob Dylan) (a)
Masters Of War (a)
Don't Think Twice (a)
Series Of Dreams
I And I

Disc two
Maggie's Farm
Band introductions during song's end
Man In A Long Black Coat
It Ain't Me Babe (a)

Filler
I'll Remember You (Not listed on cover) (1)
You're A Big Girl Now (Not listed on cover) (2)
If You See Her Say Hello (3)
Shooting Star (4)
Born In Time (5)
To Ramona (4) (a)
God Knows (3)
Blowin' In The Wind (2)

source: 
    Kosei-nenkin Hall, Hiroshima, Japan, Feb. 16, 1994 
(1) Kosei-nenkin Hall, Hiroshima, Japan, Feb. 15, 1994
(2) NHK Hall, Tokyo, Japan  February 20, 1994
(3) Bunka Taiikukan, Yokohama, Japan Feb.7, 1994
(4) Century Hall, Nagoya, Japan  Feb. 11, 1994
(5) Cultural Center, Urawa, Japan  Feb. 18, 1994

(a) acoustic



Review:
This is a great recording of an historic concert. Dylan performs some long absent jewels during this first tour of Japan in nearly a decade. The audience recording is a touch dark, but it's well mixed. Bob's vocals are slightly low in the mix on the softest words, but the overall effect is that they are well represented, and not above the music. Thanks to the always polite and respectful Japanese audience, even the softest lyrics are well heard. The highlight tonight is a breathtaking acoustic version of Masters Of War. The first time in over 30 years! Also included are some great filler highlights of some of the other rarities performed during the February Japanese tour. The sound quality is similar throughout, but in-between song splices of the filler material are cold cut, and amateurish. R.E. has created a package worthy of the event. The awe inspiring "St. Robert" cartoon graces the front cover of this classy set. Truly, "Beauty walks a Razor's Edge"
===================
===================
===================
